subject: english
topic_title: Reading to Paraphrase, Phrasal Verbs, and Informal Letter
learning_objectives: Summarize the key ideas in Reading to Paraphrase a Prose Passage.
Apply Phrasal Verbs correctly in sentences.
Write a clear Features and Format of an Informal Letter using the expected features and structure.

sections.heading: Comprehension: Reading to Paraphrase a Prose Passage
sections.content: A paraphrase is saying or rewriting something in different words that makes it simpler. In a summary, you pick out the essence; in a paraphrase, you try to re-express the same information in a simpler, less complicated, less figurative form. The passage, Nazzruddin, reveals his plans to put up his property for sale using business terms.

sections.heading: Structure: Phrasal Verbs
sections.content: Verbs often combine with adverbial particles to form multi – word verbs or phrasal verbs. The meaning cannot be determined from the verb and particle in isolation. Examples: Turndown (refuse), Give in (surrender), Run across (meet by chance), Fall on (attack), Fall out (quarrel), Give up (stop). Double particles: do away with (abolish), put up with (tolerate).

sections.heading: Features and Format of an Informal Letter
sections.content: Informal letters are friendly letters to contemporaries, close older relatives and pen-pals. Features: chatty, language must be very familiar, use short forms (I'm, can't), inquire after welfare, send greetings in conclusion. Format: Address, Date, Salutation (My dear...), Introductory paragraph, Body in paragraphs, Conclusion, Subscription (Yours sincerely), First name only.
